See the full announcement ([https://www.zintellect.com/Opportunity/Details/NIH-NIAID-2020-0004](https://www.zintellect.com/Opportunity/Details/NIH-NIAID-2020-0004)) for position description, other eligibility requirements and application procedure. 
>

## 2019-12-23

**Sean Davis** (10:19:32):
> From deCODE Genetics ([https://www.decode.com/](https://www.decode.com/)):  deCODE Genetics is searching for highly-qualified and motivated post-doctoral fellows in the fields of Computer Science, Mathematics, Biostatistics, Bioinformatics, Artificial Intelligence, Biomedical Engineering, Electrical Engineering and Statistical Genetics. This is a rare opportunity to conduct cutting-edge human population genetics research in an environment that combines the benefits of academia and industry. Post-doctoral fellows will be mentored by Dr. Kári Stefánsson and other deCODE scientific leaders. Opportunities will be available to initiate and/or participate in publications in highly-regarded journals. The tenure for these competitive fellowships is 3-years with the option to renew for an additional year. Current graduates and graduate students nearing completion of their doctoral studies are welcome to apply. Immigration and relocation assistance will be provided. The working language at deCODE is English.[https://careers.amgen.com/ShowJob/JobId/391176/PostdoctoralFellowHumanPopulationGeneticsResearch](https://careers.amgen.com/ShowJob/JobId/391176/PostdoctoralFellowHumanPopulationGeneticsResearch)
  - Attachment (decode.com): [20frontpage](https://www.decode.com/)

## 2020-02-05

**Sean Davis** (16:08:49):
> [https://unc.peopleadmin.com/postings/176636](https://unc.peopleadmin.com/postings/176636)
  - Attachment (unc.peopleadmin.com): [Bioinformatics Scientist](https://unc.peopleadmin.com/postings/176636)
    > We are seeking a highly skilled computational biologist, with experience in high-throughput sequencing technologies, for a position within the UNC Neuroscience Center. The individual recruited for this position will be expected to develop new computational pipelines, contribute to the analysis and biological interpretation of genomic data, and play an integral role in supporting multiple long- and short-term projects from design through publication. Expertise in summarizing genome-wide data as well as generating figures and tables for publications is also expected. This position requires experience in a Linux computing cluster environment, management of large datasets, data mining, bioinformatics and genomics techniques, and strong scientific communication skills.

## 2020-03-31

**Lori Shepherd** (11:13:51):
> We are looking for people interested in working full-time, or as interns or post-docs, with the Bioconductor Core Team at Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center in Buffalo!
> 
> Bioconductor ([https://bioconductor.org](https://bioconductor.org)) is a large collection of R software packages for the statistical analysis and comprehension of high-throughput genomic data. Bioconductor is open source and open development, with more than 1800 R packages written and contributed by the core team and more than 1200 domain experts from around the world. Bioconductor is widely used (more than 1/2 million unique IP downloads annually) and highly respected (35,000 PubMedCentral citations); it is supported by several federal grants, with long-term financial stability.
> 
> Individual projects are flexible but offer unique opportunities to develop novel algorithms and other aspects of scientific software to support high-throughput genomic analysis. The work is in R, with additional opportunities in C, C++ , workflow, and other programming paradigms. Bioconductor infrastructure systems are often integrated though ruby, python, bash, AWS, etc., which also provide opportunity for applicants looking for system administration, devops, or web development work. Individuals with an interest in scalable cloud-based analysis of large public genomic data resources are particularly encouraged to apply.
> 
> Please contact[Martin.Morgan@RoswellPark.org](mailto:Martin.Morgan@RoswellPark.org)for more information.
> Thank you!
> Posted on  behalf of Martin Morgan

**Lori Shepherd** (11:27:06):
> A full-time position application can also be found:[https://www.roswellpark.org/careers/research/scientific-software-developer-7283](https://www.roswellpark.org/careers/research/scientific-software-developer-7283)
  - Attachment (Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center): [Scientific Software Developer](https://www.roswellpark.org/careers/research/scientific-software-developer-7283)
    > Health Research, Inc. (HRI) Roswell Park Division is seeking a full-time Scientific Software Developer. This position involves challenging and creative opportunities for a talented and independent scientist to contribute to novel algorithm and other software developments to support high-throughput genomic analyses. The position is with the widely used and respected Bioconductor project ([https://bioconductor.org](https://bioconductor.org)) for the analysis and comprehension of high throughput genomic data. The work will be in R, with additional opportunities in C, C++ , workflow, and other programming paradigms as needed for projects identified and pursued by the successful applicant. Individuals with an interest in scalable cloud-based analysis and comprehension of large public genomic data are particularly encouraged to apply. The position also involves participation in day-to-day project activities, including new package review, maintaining internal and contributed packages, and contribution to training and outreach activities.

## 2020-09-05

**Sean Davis** (12:18:41):
> > If you know any PhD-level Bioinformatics/Computational Biologists that would be interested in leading projects through the University of Colorado Cancer Center, please share the following job ad widely. Thanks for your consideration and happy to answer any questions.[https://cu.taleo.net/careersection/2/jobdetail.ftl?job=19137&lang=en](https://cu.taleo.net/careersection/2/jobdetail.ftl?job=19137&lang=en)The University of Colorado Comprehensive Cancer Center (UCCC) is seeking a PhD-level Bioinformatics researcher who will specialize in the analysis of –omics data. The UCCC places a strong emphasis on reproducible research, and a skillset that is compatible with reproducible data science practices is required. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record of leading the analysis of experiments using multiple -omic profiling technologies, such as whole genome/exome sequencing, ChIP-/ATAC-seq, RNAseq, proteomics, metabolomics, mass cytometry, and/or single cell technologies. The holder of this position will collaborate with UCCC investigators to lead the bioinformatics analysis of individual projects. This position will also participate in group meetings with other Bioinformaticists and occasionally present in educational seminars. An additional benefit of this position will be the ability to spend up to 25% of their time developing novel computational methods as independent research. Thus, the individual will have the opportunity to develop independence of research through individual and collaborative grant funding.
> Cheers,
> Jim
> 
> Jim Costello, PhD
> Associate Professor
> Department of Pharmacology
> Boettcher Investigator
> University of Colorado Anschutz Medical Campus
> Mail Stop 8303
> 12801 E. 17th Ave., Rm L18-6114
> Aurora, CO 80045[(303) 724-8619](tel:3037248619)[james.costello@cuanschutz.edu](mailto:james.costello@cuanschutz.edu)
  - Attachment (cu.taleo.net): [Research Associate](https://cu.taleo.net/careersection/2/jobdetail.ftl?job=19137&amp;lang=en)
    > Click the link provided to see the complete job description.

## 2020-10-05

**Sean Davis** (08:28:39):
> Bioconductor and the Channing Division of Network Medicine at Harvard Medical School have created a new project management/software developer/manager position. We are seeking candidates. Join an influential open source software project, interact with great scientists, and help manage a distributed, highly capable team and drive the Bioconductor project and Harvard science forward.[https://bit.ly/3lfWZtC](https://bit.ly/3lfWZtC)This position includes both project management and advanced software development. The successful applicant will perform multidisciplinary work in data science, clinical trials, and cloud computing. Our group is centrally involved with the Bioconductor project, a 20-year open source/open development effort in the domain of statistics and computing for genome scale biology. Strong acquaintance with github, OpenAPI, R, Linux, Amazon Web Services, or Google Cloud Platform expected.
> 
> - Understand the software ecosystem defined at[bioconductor.org](http://bioconductor.org), with particular attention to the build system, system health reporting, and release cycle for approximately 2000 R packages. All packages are built on Linux, Windows, and Mac platforms.
> - Understand the Terra cloud computing platform underlying[anvilproject.org](http://anvilproject.org), and the OpenAPI/Swagger definitions of platform API components.
> - Participate in the training of physician-scientists and professional data scientists in the operation of Bioconductor and cloud computing platforms for computational research in biology and clinical applications.
> - Produce R packages for addressing newly posed problems in genomics and epidemiology arising in the research portfolio of the Channing Division of Network Medicine.
> - Manage the work of one or more junior bioinformaticians.
  - Attachment (partners.taleo.net): [IS Project Manager/ Day/ 40 Hrs/ Channing Division of Network Medicine](https://bit.ly/3lfWZtC)
    > Click the link provided to see the complete job description.

## 2020-10-14

**Artem Sokolov** (10:54:11):
> I am posting this on behalf of Robert Gentleman. The newly established Center for Computational Biomedicine at Harvard Medical School is looking to fill***two positions***. Both positions are at the Research Associate rank. Please contact Susanne Churchill ([Susanne_Churchill@hms.harvard.edu](mailto:Susanne_Churchill@hms.harvard.edu)) if interested.
  - File (PDF): [CCB_RA.pdf](https://community-bioc.slack.com/files/U34P8RS3B/F01CH07K37X/ccb_ra.pdf)

**Ludwig Geistlinger** (16:04:03) (in thread):
> Is there an official job posting describing required application documents, job starting date, and salary range?


## 2020-10-15

**Artem Sokolov** (10:03:44) (in thread):
> Hi@Ludwig Geistlinger. I reached out to Susanne and got the following response:
> > Please share these related bits of info:
> 
> 1.        The leader of this new effort, the HMS Center for Computational Biomedicine or CCB, is Dr. Robert Gentleman, co-inventor of the coding language “R”, a veteran of academic and business innovation using AI and ML approaches, and most recently lead computationalist at 23 and Me.
> 2.       Salary will depend on candidate experience and training but is**anticipated**to fall into the $100-125K range; note this is a brand new enterprise so this part truly is a “work in progress” regarding provisioning of a skilled cadre of bioinformaticians/computer scientists to bring cutting edge “data science” approaches to our basic science research efforts.
> 3.       These first two “foundational” staff positions represent the beginning of an intellectual nexus for data scientists that is expected to grow as their impact is fully realized. The CCB will be building a unique cadre of computationally savvy professionals whose focus will be enhancement of HMS’s overall research portfolio.
> 4.       A letter of interest and CV should be submitted to[Susanne_churchill@hms.harvard.edu](mailto:Susanne_churchill@hms.harvard.edu); these positions are immediately available and will be filled as soon as possible.
> 5.       A CCB website is under development and will shortly have a banner page on the HMS Department of Biomedical Informatics website
> 6.       Questions/inquiries welcome.

**Ludwig Geistlinger** (10:04:55) (in thread):
> Thanks for the follow-up@Artem Sokolov!

## 2020-10-26

**Hector Climente** (06:16:56):
> New***postdoc/research***scientist position in the High-dimensional Statistical Modeling Team, at RIKEN AIP, Japan. We are focusing on developing***machine learning***algorithms for the large-feature small-number samples and solve important societal problems. Importantly, we focus on applying them to biological problems. We are currently interested in***deep learning***models for high-dimensional and small sample data [1], which is a fundamental and challenging problem for the biological domain. We also work on various machine learning algorithms, including graph neural networks (GNNs) [2] and***optimal transport***[3].
> [1] FsNet: Feature Selection Network on High-dimensional Biological Data[https://arxiv.org/abs/2001.08322](https://arxiv.org/abs/2001.08322)[2] Approximation Ratios of Graph Neural Networks for Combinatorial Problems,[https://papers.nips.cc/paper/8662-approximation-ratios-of-graph-neural-networks-for-combinatorial-problems](https://papers.nips.cc/paper/8662-approximation-ratios-of-graph-neural-networks-for-combinatorial-problems)[3] Tree-Sliced Variants of Wasserstein Distances[http://papers.nips.cc/paper/9396-tree-sliced-variants-of-wasserstein-distances.pdf](http://papers.nips.cc/paper/9396-tree-sliced-variants-of-wasserstein-distances.pdf)Information About the RIKEN High-dimensional Statistical Modeling Team:
> * We are part of RIKEN, Japan’s largest multidisciplinary research institution, and enclaved in the historical Kyoto University.
> * We are based in beautiful Kyoto ([https://kyoto.travel/en/](https://kyoto.travel/en/)), and close to Osaka, the second largest city in Japan.
> * We are an international team: both our members and collaborators are from all over the world.
> * We are well funded: the candidate can fully focus on research.
> * We regularly publish papers at top ML venues, including NeurIPS, ICML, AISTATS, and ICLR ([https://riken-yamada.github.io/publication.html](https://riken-yamada.github.io/publication.html)).
> Job description
> * A PhD in maths / ML / bioinformatics or similar is required. Experience in biology will be highly valued.
> * Develop machine learning models for high-dimensional, real-world applications, especially in biology.
> * Pursue your own scientific interests in a highly collaborative environment.
> * Write research papers and develop software in R or Python.
> * Salary is competitive, and RIKEN offers good benefits
> * (For research scientist applicants) Mentor younger scientists.
> Here is the official site for applications:[https://www.riken.jp/en/careers/researchers/20201016_4/index.html](https://www.riken.jp/en/careers/researchers/20201016_4/index.html)Feel free to contact me, or our PI ([makoto.yamada@riken.jp](mailto:makoto.yamada@riken.jp)) if you have any question:slightly_smiling_face:

**Aedin Culhane** (20:45:19):
> Lori is this it?[https://www.roswellpark.org/careers/research/scientific-software-developer-7283](https://www.roswellpark.org/careers/research/scientific-software-developer-7283)
  - Attachment (Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center): [Scientific Software Developer](https://www.roswellpark.org/careers/research/scientific-software-developer-7283)
    > Health Research, Inc. (HRI) Roswell Park Division is seeking a full-time Scientific Software Developer. This position involves challenging and creative opportunities for a talented and independent scientist to contribute to novel algorithm and other software developments to support high-throughput genomic analyses. The position is with the widely used and respected Bioconductor project ([https://bioconductor.org](https://bioconductor.org)) for the analysis and comprehension of high throughput genomic data. The work will be in R, with additional opportunities in C, C++ , workflow, and other programming paradigms as needed for projects identified and pursued by the successful applicant. Individuals with an interest in scalable cloud-based analysis and comprehension of large public genomic data are particularly encouraged to apply. The position also involves participation in day-to-day project activities, including new package review, maintaining internal and contributed packages, and contribution to training and outreach activities.

**Robert Ivánek** (10:20:00):
> *_Bioinformatician in Experimental Cancer Research (80-100%)_*
> 
> The [Childhood leukemia research group]([https://biomedizin.unibas.ch/en/research/research-groups/schwaller-lab/](https://biomedizin.unibas.ch/en/research/research-groups/schwaller-lab/)) is seeking a highly motivated member for our team at the Department of Biomedicine of the University Children’s Hospital in Basel/Switzerland.
> 
> We are looking for a bioinformatician to analyze different datasets (RNA-Seq, scRNA-Seq, ChIP-Seq, ATAC-Seq, Proteomics) emerging from our models for blood cancer. You will work in close collaboration with the [DBM Bioinformatics Core Facility]([https://www.sib.swiss/robert-ivanek-group](https://www.sib.swiss/robert-ivanek-group)).
> 
> Ideally, you have a Master-, or PhD degree in quantitative field such as computational biology, physics or mathematics with strong interest in biology and you have proven practical experience in analysis of biological datasets such as NGS.
> Sound statistical knowledge and fluent programming skills in R/Bioconductor are required.
> Interest and active contribution of supervision and guidance of (under) graduate students is expected.
> 
> The Department of Biomedicine of the University of Basel hosts over 60 different research groups and offers an international stimulating, dynamic and friendly atmosphere in an attractive city, rich in culture, located in the middle of Europe.
> More information can be obtained at: [[https://biomedizin.unibas.ch/en/home/\](https://biomedizin.unibas.ch/en/home/)](https://biomedizin.unibas.ch/en/home/](https://biomedizin.unibas.ch/en/home/)).
> 
> For applications and further inquiries please contact:
> 
> Prof. Jürg Schwaller
> Childhood leukemia group
> University Children’s Hospital (UKBB)
> Department of Biomedicine, University of Basel
> Zentrum für Lehre und Forschung (ZLF)
> Hebelstrasse 20
> CH-4031 Basel, Switzerland
> Email: J.Schwaller 'at'unibas.ch

## 2021-03-08

*05:35:20 - @Calli Dendrou has joined the channel*
**Calli Dendrou** (05:37:46):
> ***Come join an exciting collaboration as and Oxford-Janssen Single Cell Genomics Senior Bioinformatician!***[https://my.corehr.com/pls/uoxrecruit/erq_jobspec_version_4.display_form?p_company=1\[…\]display_apply_ind=Y&p_refresh_search=Y&p_recruitment_id=150015](https://my.corehr.com/pls/uoxrecruit/erq_jobspec_version_4.display_form?p_company=10&p_internal_external=I&p_display_in_irish=N&p_process_type=&p_applicant_no=&p_form_profile_detail=&p_display_apply_ind=Y&p_refresh_search=Y&p_recruitment_id=150015)Grade 8: Salary range of £41,526 - £44,045 per annum
> Applications are invited for a Oxford-Janssen Single Cell Genomics Senior Bioinformatician to join  Dr Calliope Dendrou’s group at the Wellcome Centre for Human Genetics. This is a unique opportunity to help lead the data analysis for rewarding research that is making a fundamental impact on human health.
> As a Oxford-Janssen Single Cell Senior Bioinformatician, you will join the Oxford-Janssen Cartography project that aims to provide insights into pathogenic pathways across immune mediated inflammatory disorders. You will thrive on scientific challenges associated with analysing complex biological data sets, as well as testing and developing new analytical tools and methods. You will produce manuscript-level reports and take the lead in more specialised publications on bioinformatics. You will also communicate with a range of stakeholders, including the Oxford-Janssen management team and contributing groups across Oxford and Janssen.
> You will hold a PhD/ DPhil in Bioinformatics, Statistics, Mathematics or another related computational subject. You will have demonstrable experience of analysing single-cell RNA-seq data (generated using 10X Genomics platforms) derived from human tissue. You will also have a proficiency with a high-level programming language (Python, R) and/or appropriate scripting languages. It is essential that you have previous experience in a leadership role and driving project completion. You will also have excellent interpersonal skills, the capacity to work collaboratively and to communicate results clearly and effectively.
> The position is for a fixed-term contract for 24 months, in the first instance, and is funded by Janssen.
> Informal inquiries about this post can be addressed to Dr Calliope Dendrou[cdendrou@well.ox.ac.uk](mailto:cdendrou@well.ox.ac.uk)Applications for this vacancy are to be made online. You will be required to upload a CV and supporting statement as part of your online application.
> Only applications received before 12.00 midday on 25 March 2021 can be considered. Please quote reference 150015 on all correspondence.


## 2021-03-10

**Elana Fertig** (09:41:20):
> FYI

**Elana Fertig** (09:41:20):
> Dear Elana, 
> 
> I hope all is well. We are looking for two postdocs within the broad areas of vaccine research, B-cell receptor sequencing, epitope prediction, and machine learning: we have 1 experimental and 1 computational position available. The positions are part of the (i) University of Oslo Scientia Fellow scheme, (ii) an EU grant on vaccine research, (iii) in collaboration with a large pharmaceutical company.
>  
> Experimental position:[https://www.med.uio.no/english/research/scientia-fellows/thematic-areas/immunology/](https://www.med.uio.no/english/research/scientia-fellows/thematic-areas/immunology/)(main supervision:[Gunnveig Grødeland](https://nam02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.med.uio.no%2Fklinmed%2Fenglish%2Fresearch%2Fgroups%2Finfluenza-adaptive-immunity%2Findex.html&data=04%7C01%7Cejfertig%40jhmi.edu%7C581d6a5b69b144b44c9c08d8e3d22ce8%7C9fa4f438b1e6473b803f86f8aedf0dec%7C0%7C0%7C637509839128676257%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C1000&sdata=vgrmRv%2BBgi18neG0jSf5w3G5X2UB%2B8LUN3qpjUMe7Cs%3D&reserved=0))
> Computational position:[https://www.med.uio.no/english/research/scientia-fellows/thematic-areas/immunoinformatics/](https://www.med.uio.no/english/research/scientia-fellows/thematic-areas/immunoinformatics/)[ 5](https://nam02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.med.uio.no%2Fenglish%2Fresearch%2Fscientia-fellows%2Fthematic-areas%2Fimmunoinformatics%2F&data=04%7C01%7Cejfertig%40jhmi.edu%7C581d6a5b69b144b44c9c08d8e3d22ce8%7C9fa4f438b1e6473b803f86f8aedf0dec%7C0%7C0%7C637509839128676257%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C1000&sdata=o%2F0xLmrkGDwcc4C%2FTi4TK4Ac1teiSenUq43nsRMF624%3D&reserved=0)(main supervision:[Victor Greiff](https://nam02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.greifflab.org%2F&data=04%7C01%7Cejfertig%40jhmi.edu%7C581d6a5b69b144b44c9c08d8e3d22ce8%7C9fa4f438b1e6473b803f86f8aedf0dec%7C0%7C0%7C637509839128686251%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C1000&sdata=x5s1i%2BB6ilRKLr7p1gMTkClG%2B66mKP8b3nGl4MJVits%3D&reserved=0))
> Scientia fellow program:[https://www.med.uio.no/english/research/scientia-fellows/about/](https://www.med.uio.no/english/research/scientia-fellows/about/)[ 1](https://nam02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.med.uio.no%2Fenglish%2Fresearch%2Fscientia-fellows%2Fabout%2F&data=04%7C01%7Cejfertig%40jhmi.edu%7C581d6a5b69b144b44c9c08d8e3d22ce8%7C9fa4f438b1e6473b803f86f8aedf0dec%7C0%7C0%7C637509839128686251%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C1000&sdata=8235wC6g9zwix%2FDC%2B7zRoTWYsG2%2Flad4flpKircEAh8%3D&reserved=0)I would greatly appreciate if you could pass it on to potentially interested candidates.   - Attachment (roche.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com): [Software Engineer / Data scientist](https://roche.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com/ROG-A2O-GENE/job/South-San-Francisco/Software-Engineer---Data-scientist_202103-107694-1)
    > We are seeking a highly motivated Software engineer / Data scientist  to join our Data Science and Statistical Computing group in Genentech Research. This role will involve developing new bioinformatics software tools for interactive analyses of genomics data and related technologies, with a focus on their applicability to target and biomarker discovery. This role requires strong technical and communication skills as well as familiarity with bioinformatics. Responsibilities will include: Creating performant, user-friendly and interoperable implementations of algorithms and statistical methods for genomics data analysis. Developing end-to-end workflows for statistical analyses of genomics data, typically spanning multiple software tools and methods. Contributing to the development of user-friendly data structures to represent new data modalities in interactive analysis environments. Collaborating with key stakeholders for development of bespoke analysis pipelines in emerging scientific domains. Successful candidates will meet many of the following requirements: MS or PhD in bioinformatics, biology,  statistics, or other scientific field High level of proficiency in a high-level data science language like R or Python, including development of reusable packages/modules Experience with bioinformatics frameworks like Bioconductor or scipy/numpy Strong statistical software engineering skills and Unix/Linux proficiency Experience with deploying workflows on shared high-performance and/or cloud computing environments Experience with standard bioinformatics workflows (e.g, read alignment, quantification, differential gene expression, single-cell analyses) Genentech provides an exceptional research environment composed of internationally recognized leaders, state of the art core facilities with cutting edge technologies, and a drive for innovation. We foster a highly inclusive and collaborative community, and as such, you will interact closely with talented engineers, leading scientists, clinical development teams and others, all working together to deliver life saving therapies to patients. #gREDinformatics #RA1

## 2021-04-05

**Sean Davis** (11:19:01):
> [https://cu.taleo.net/careersection/2/jobdetail.ftl?job=20657](https://cu.taleo.net/careersection/2/jobdetail.ftl?job=20657)***University of Colorado School of Medicine******Department of Biochemistry and Molecular Genetics******Center for Health Artificial Intelligence******Open Rank Faculty (Assistant Professor – Professor)******Position number 00782388******Nature of Work***The Center for Health Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the Department of Biochemistry and Molecular Genetics at the University of Colorado School of Medicine invite applications for open rank faculty positions in the areas of biomedical informatics, knowledge engineering, and machine learning. The new faculty member will join a highly interactive, supportive, and interdisciplinary group of faculty, postdoctoral fellows, and students and enjoy access to state-of-the-art equipment and facilities on the Anschutz Medical Campus. The primary duties of the faculty member are to develop a research program of national and international importance and to mentor and educate trainees and students.
> Faculty in the Center for Health AI focus on computational approaches that derive insights from data to lead to advances in research and medical practice. The Center for Health AI is collocated on the Anschutz Medical Campus with the UC Health University of Colorado Hospital, Colorado Childrens, and Rocky Mountain Regional VA Medical Center. The Anschutz Campus is home to Colorado’s only Comprehensive Cancer Center and the Colorado Clinical and Translational Sciences Institute. The campus includes a vibrant and entrepreneurial basic research community with multiple areas of excellence and translational impact. There are many opportunities on campus to translate data intensive research advances into improved care. The campus has made a significant investment in an enterprise health data warehouse collecting electronic health record data from 10 member hospitals and dozens of clinics available for research. Partnerships with precision medicine programs associated with both adult and pediatric care communities can be entertained for hires under this posting. Candidates who would like help identifying potential campus partners in advance of an application are encouraged to reach out to Audrey Wen ([AUDREY.WEN@CUANSCHUTZ.EDU](mailto:AUDREY.WEN@CUANSCHUTZ.EDU)) with a one paragraph description of research interests and a CV.
> Within the areas defined above, the search is not specific to a single application area or analytical approach. The committee is aware of opportunities for strategic partnerships in areas such as multi-modal data integration, bibliometrics, automated and manual biocuration, knowledge representation, semantic engineering, transfer learning or domain adaptation, privacy and bias assessement, or automated reasoning are encouraged to highlight such methodological activities in their application. Candidates with a particular interest in commercialization and technology transfer should also note this in their cover letter.
  - Attachment (cu.taleo.net): [Open Rank](https://cu.taleo.net/careersection/2/jobdetail.ftl?job=20657)
    > Click the link provided to see the complete job description.

## 2021-07-16

**Artem Sokolov** (17:47:16):
> From Brian White:
> > The Jackson Laboratory (JAX) is seeking a Computational Scientist to join a single-cell imaging and informatics team embedded within the Computational Sciences group. The successful candidate will apply statistical, machine learning, and deep learning approaches to predict phenotypes in cancer and non-malignant disease from imaging and spatial expression data generated by cutting-edge technologies within JAX’s Single Cell Biology Laboratory (SCBL). These platforms include spatial transcriptomics, which provides unbiased, spatially-resolved expression profiles of mixtures of cells, and imaging mass cytometry (IMC), which affords a highly multiplexed (~40 proteins) perspective of individual cells. SCBL’s platforms continue to evolve and will soon include CODEX and multiplexFISH. The primary responsibilities of the Computational Scientist involve providing analysis of tissue imaging data and interpreting their results across a range of biological and translational domains. The Computational Scientist will apply and optimize published methods and pipelines when they exist and develop novel methods when they do not. In doing so, they will accelerate adoption of advanced imaging and spatially-aware technologies at JAX.
> ****[Full and partial remote work options are available]****Full posting:[https://thejacksonlaboratory.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/External_JAX/job/Farmington-Connecticut/Computa\[…\]l-Scientist--Assistant-Associate-Full-_JR000780](https://thejacksonlaboratory.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/External_JAX/job/Farmington-Connecticut/Computational-Scientist--Assistant-Associate-Full-_JR000780)For questions and additional information, contact Brian White at[Brian.White@jax.org](mailto:Brian.White@jax.org)


## 2021-07-19

**Jared Andrews** (11:09:05):
> Open Bioinformatics Research Scientist position in the Mefford Lab at St. Jude Children's Research Hospital studying neurological diseases:
> > The[ Mefford Lab](https://www.stjude.org/directory/m/heather-mefford.html)in the Center for Pediatric Neurological Disease Research at St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital uses genome-wide approaches to identify genetic, genomic and epigenetic causes of human disease including epilepsy, and related pediatric neurodevelopmental disorders. The major focus of our current work is to identify causes of pediatric epilepsy by employing state-of-the-art technologies including whole exome sequencing, targeted gene panel sequencing and custom array comparative genomic hybridization (aCGH). The Mefford lab has discovered numerous new epilepsy genes and copy number variants, including deletions of chromosomes 1q21, 15q13 and 17q12, each of which cause a range of clinical features.
> Full posting:[https://careers-stjude.icims.com/jobs/7365/bioinformatics-research-scientist--neurologica\[…\]true&needsRedirect=false&jan1offset=-360&jun1offset=-300](https://careers-stjude.icims.com/jobs/7365/bioinformatics-research-scientist--neurological-disease/job?mobile=false&width=1180&height=500&bga=true&needsRedirect=false&jan1offset=-360&jun1offset=-300)

## 2021-08-24

**Qian Liu** (14:16:24):
> [https://roswellpark.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/ExternalCareers/job/Research-Studies-Center-RSC/Senior-Biostatistician_R-11431](https://roswellpark.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/ExternalCareers/job/Research-Studies-Center-RSC/Senior-Biostatistician_R-11431)
  - Attachment (roswellpark.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com): [Senior Bioinformatician](https://roswellpark.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/ExternalCareers/job/Research-Studies-Center-RSC/Senior-Biostatistician_R-11431)
    > Title: Senior Bioinformatician Company:Health Research Inc. Department: Biostatistics & Bioinformatics Time Type: Full time Weekly Hours: 40 Shift: First Shift (United States of America) Summary: The Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics at Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center is seeking a highly talented bioinformatics research scientist to work in Dr. Lei Wei’s lab ([https://www.roswellpark.org/lei-wei](https://www.roswellpark.org/lei-wei)). The scientist will primarily work on an NIH-funded project focusing on skin cancer prevention and early detection, with a specific focus on cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma (CSCC). CSCC is the second most common cancer in the US. However, there is limited understanding of the CSCC’s initiation, progression, and preventative treatment efficacy. The rise of CSCC is driven by the clonal mutation (CM) in the skin. Previously Dr. Wei’s group has developed bioinformatics methods for accurate detection of CM in the normal skin before CSCC arises. The next step is to identify the relationship between the accumulation of CMs and cancer development. The scientist will join an interdisciplinary team of bioinformaticians, clinicians, computer scientists, and molecular biologists, who are working together to develop novel methods for quantitatively measuring SCC development and evaluating the efficacy of preventative strategies used in the clinic. The candidate will have the opportunity to access various leading-edge genomics datasets and interact with the investigators in Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center for collaborative and independent research. Core responsibilities • Apply statistical or machine learning approaches to identify molecular biomarkers for tumor development • Develop innovative computational approaches and/or evaluate existing methods for multi-omics cancer genomics data • Data visualization and creation of data figures to be presented in scientific publications • Performs other job-related duties as assigned or requested Qualifications:Required Education and Experience Master’s degree in Statistics, Biostatistics, Biometry or a related field and the equivalent of two (2) years of full-time experience in Biostatistics or Biomathematics Consulting. NOTE: Required degrees must have been granted by an accredited college or university or one recognized by Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center as following acceptable educational practices. Preferred Qualifications: A minimum of 2 years of post-degree experience with a PhD (or 4 years of relevant work experience without a PhD) Strong programming skill with any of R, Python, Java, Perl, C/C++, or other programming languages, under Linux/Unix environment Experience in cancer or genomics research Experience in statistics or computer science (machine learning) Roswell Park Cancer Institute Corporation (RPCIC) and Health Research Inc. (HRI) Roswell Park Division believe that all persons are entitled to equal employment opportunities, and we do not discriminate against our employees, applicants or job seekers because of their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, creed, age, disability, pregnancy-related condition, military or veteran status, marital or familial status, domestic violence victim status, citizenship status, genetic information, individual’s relationship or association with a member of a protected category or any other protected group status as defined by law. Additionally, RPCIC and HRI Roswell Park Division are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to individuals with disabilities. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the employment process, please send an e-mail to HR-Benefits@RoswellPark.org or call [(716) 845-4700](tel:(716)845-4700) and let us know the nature of your request and your contact information. This is a grant-funded position.  Compliance with funding requirements such as time and effort reporting, grant deliverables, and contract deliverables is required. Health Research Incorporated (HRI) Roswell Park Division participates in the E-Verify program. Affirmative Action/Equal Opportunity Employer/Qualified Individuals with Disabilities/Qualified Protected Veterans

**Qian Liu** (14:17:23):
> :point_up_2:Remote eligible.

## 2021-09-22

**Chiaowen Joyce Hsiao** (07:24:16):
> Moderna is hiring a statistical genomics co-op in 2022 to work on single-cell genomics applications in immunology (masters or PhD with experiences in single-cell omics data analysis, method development). Please free to message me if you have any questions about the position.[https://modernatx.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/en-US/M_tx/job/Remote---US/Co-op--Computational-Science--Statistical-Genomics_R5989](https://modernatx.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/en-US/M_tx/job/Remote---US/Co-op--Computational-Science--Statistical-Genomics_R5989)
  - Attachment (modernatx.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com): [Co-op, Computational Science, Statistical Genomics](https://modernatx.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/en-US/M_tx/job/Remote---US/Co-op--Computational-Science--Statistical-Genomics_R5989)
    > The Role: Moderna is seeking a talented and highly motivated master-level student with a background in genomics, computational biology, bioinformatics, computer science, applicated statistics or related fields, excellent communication skills, and a deep interest in drug discovery and development. The successful candidate will work closely with a senior statistician on developing and collaborate with immunologists, biologists and computational scientists on single-cell genomics applications for drug discover and development. Here’s What You’ll Do: Work as a part of Computational Science Review and benchmark computational methods and tools for multimodal data integration, cell-cell communication modeling, cell type assignment, etc. Participate in designing single-cell sequencing experiments that address challenges specific to development mRNA therapeutics Work collaboratively with statisticians and immunologists to carry out single-cell multiomics data analysis Here’s What You’ll Bring to the Table: Master-level student with a background in genomics, computational biology, bioinformatics, computer science, applicated statistics or related fields. Scientific and technical expertise in the following: Some experiences with bioinformatics pipelines of next-generation sequencing data Experiences with single-cell RNA-seq bioinformatics workflow and data analysis are preferred but not required Proficiency in R and Python Working knowledge of Git version control and AWS Strong interests in real-world problems and telling stories with data Strong written and oral communication skills in communicating technical knowledge to a non-expert audience Strong interests in working with a multidisciplinary team consisting of statisticians, bioinformaticians, molecular biologists, chemists, and immunologists. Here’s What We’ll Bring to the Table: On-site subsidized cafeteria or catered lunches Free parking, monthly subway pass or a subsidized commuter rail pass Free annual corporate membership to Bluebikes Highly competitive healthcare coverage including: medical offered through BCBS (HMO/PPO), dental, and vision offered through VSP Flexible Spending Accounts for medical expenses and dependent care expenses Candidates should be aware that Moderna currently maintains a policy requiring all US-based employees to be fully vaccinated against COVID-19.  Newly hired employees must be vaccinated prior to their employment start date.  Moderna is an equal opportunity employer and will provide a reasonable accommodation to those unable to be vaccinated where it is not an undue hardship to the company to do so as provided under federal, state, and local law. About Moderna: In 10 years since its inception, Moderna has transformed from a science research-stage company advancing programs in the field of messenger RNA (mRNA), to an enterprise with a diverse clinical portfolio of vaccines and therapeutics across six modalities, a broad intellectual property portfolio in areas including mRNA and lipid nanoparticle formulation, and an integrated manufacturing plant that allows for both clinical and commercial production at scale and at unprecedented speed. Moderna maintains alliances with a broad range of domestic and overseas government and commercial collaborators, which has allowed for the pursuit of both groundbreaking science and rapid scaling of manufacturing. Most recently, Moderna’s capabilities have come together to allow the authorized use of one of the earliest and most effective vaccines against the COVID-19 pandemic. Moderna’s mRNA platform builds on continuous advances in basic and applied mRNA science, delivery technology and manufacturing, and has allowed the development of therapeutics and vaccines for infectious diseases, immuno-oncology, rare diseases, cardiovascular diseases and auto-immune diseases. Moderna has been named a top biopharmaceutical employer by Science for the past six years. To learn more, visit [www.modernatx.com](http://www.modernatx.com). Moderna is a smoke-free, alcohol-free and drug-free work environment. Moderna is committed to equal employment opportunity and non-discrimination for all employees and qualified applicants without regard to a person’s race, color, gender, age, religion, national origin, ancestry, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation or any characteristic protected under applicable law. Moderna will make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with known disabilities, in accordance with applicable law. #LI-EA1 -

**Ava Hoffman (she/her)** (14:31:27):
> The Department of Biostatistics at the Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health has openings for two remote-friendly Teaching Postdoctoral Fellowships in genomics data science. These positions will focus on developing original teaching materials to help students learn genomics approaches as well as organizing and designing outreach events like workshops, hackathons, and other activities. 
> 
> The larger project is dedicated to bringing bioinformatics and data science to undergraduates and researchers who don’t have access to high performance computing and/or data science curriculum. The JHU team has already been working closely with Minority Serving Institutions and community colleges to develop curriculum, giving the fellows a chance to have a big impact. The team is also committed to helping the fellows reach their career goals following the appointment.
> 
> We are particularly interested to hear from applicants with a non-traditional background who can bring a unique perspective to the team.
> 
> Please see more application details[here](https://github.com/jtleek/jtleek.github.io/blob/master/Teaching%20Postdoctoral%20Fellowships%20in%20Genomics%20Data%20Science.pdf), and please share with anyone you think might be interested. Don’t hesitate to reach out with any questions! Review will start immediately and continue until the position is filled.

## 2021-12-17

*08:56:08 - @Kellie Kravarik has joined the channel*
**Kellie Kravarik** (08:56:43):
> Systems Immunology at Pfizer (Cambridge, MA USA)  is looking for a computational biologist to focus on multiomic, cellular, and spatial data integration and analysis, to support our remit of identifying new drug targets and supporting our pipeline. Come work with our awesome data![https://pfizer.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/en-US/PfizerCareers/job/United-States---Massachusetts---Cambridge/Senior-Computational-Scientist_4833761-1](https://pfizer.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/en-US/PfizerCareers/job/United-States---Massachusetts---Cambridge/Senior-Computational-Scientist_4833761-1)
  - Attachment (pfizer.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com): [Senior Computational Scientist](https://pfizer.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/en-US/PfizerCareers/job/United-States---Massachusetts---Cambridge/Senior-Computational-Scientist_4833761-1)
    > ROLE SUMMARY Pfizer’s Inflammation and Immunology (I&I) Research Unit is developing novel therapies that change patients’ lives. This is a unique opportunity for an exceptional scientist to apply machine learning and deep computational expertise to transforming drug discovery research. The rapid growth of high-dimensional biomedical data at all levels of granularity together with advancement in powerful computational methods offer new opportunities for science-based drug discovery. There is a great need to integrate genetics, spatial ‘omics and multi-omics data with human disease and clinical studies for better understanding of disease mechanisms, target discovery, combination therapy, and precision medicine. The Inflammation & Immunology Research Unit is capitalizing on this emerging science with an embedded and dedicated Systems Immunology group to influence all aspects of the drug discovery pipeline. If you are a research scientist with a passion for solving biomedical problems using complex, high-dimensional ‘omics data in conjunction with innovative computational approaches, then the Systems Immunology group is the team for [you.As](http://you.As) a Computational Biologist, you will be an agile and core contributor to the impactful, data-driven research in Pfizer’s R&D organization. Your core mission is to support our quest for novel targets, better therapies, and precision medicine strategies, at all stages of drug discovery in the Inflammation and Immunology Research Unit.You will apply machine learning/ deep learning and other advanced computational approaches to spatial ‘omics of human disease and perturbation studies and integration of multiple data modalities, to provide novel actionable insights for drug discovery. The role will be in close alignment with colleagues within the Systems Immunology function as well as project teams and partner lines to inform the Inflammation and Immunology drug discovery pipeline. We also expect the successful candidate to communicate the value and efficiency of new computational biology research and capabilities in order to advance awareness and implementation throughout Pfizer’s R&D. This position offers an exciting opportunity to execute science-based drug discovery at one of the world’s leading pharmaceutical companies, at the Pfizer Biomedical Institute based in the Cambridge Innovation Hub. ROLE RESPONSIBILITIES Work effectively in cross-functional teams and provide computational and statistical expertise to advancing drug discovery projects in the Inflammation & Immunology Research Unit. Apply machine learning/ deep learning and other computational approaches to analyzing spatial omics data, to decipher disease mechanisms and perturbation effects in multicellular tissue organization. Integrate spatial omics with other omics data modalities to identify novel therapeutic targets, review and validate therapeutic hypotheses, better understand disease mechanisms and causal pathways, and inform precision medicine strategies. Actively identify new opportunities to develop and implement advanced computational methods, including machine learning/ deep learning, Bayesian inference, and probabilistic modeling, for innovative research in drug discovery. Collaborate with Systems Immunology team members in performing genetics and multi-omics data analyses and integrating public and proprietary omics data resources for novel insights into disease biology and therapeutics. Communicate analyses and study findings with internal project teams, leadership, and external collaborators. Publish research findings in high quality scientific journals. Work collaboratively with computational biologists in Integrative Biology to further the development of Pfizer-wide integrated omics data analyses. Participate in collaborations with external academic and industry partners to transform our computational systems immunology toolkit and expand multi-omics data access. QUALIFICATIONS BASIC QUALIFICATIONS Ph.D. in Computational Biology, Statistics, Computer Science, Applied Mathematics, or a related discipline; 0-3 years post-PhD work experience in an academic or biopharma/biotech setting. 5+ years of relevant research experience in applying advanced computational and statistical approaches to solving biomedical problems. Strong background and experience in machine learning/ deep learning, statistical inference, probabilistic modeling, and/or causal inference. Extensive experience in analyzing multi-omics data (genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics, single-cell RNA-seq, etc.) for biomedical research. Proficient in programing languages Python and R, and machine learning and deep learning libraries. PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Solid background in human biology / medicine, with practical working knowledge of autoimmune & inflammatory diseases. Experience in analyzing spatial omics (transcriptomics or proteomics) or imaging data. Enthusiasm for solving biomedical problems, and proven ability from ideation to finding computational solutions and advancing biomedical discovery. Demonstrated track record of excellence in publications. Working experience in a pharmaceutical or biotech setting. OTHER JOB DETAILS Eligible for Relocation Package Eligible for Employee Referral Bonus Pfizer requires all U.S. new hires to be fully vaccinated for COVID-19 prior to the first date of employment.  As required by applicable law, Pfizer will consider requests for Reasonable Accommodations. Sunshine Act Pfizer reports payments and other transfers of value to health care providers as required by federal and state transparency laws and implementing regulations.  These laws and regulations require Pfizer to provide government agencies with information such as a health care provider’s name, address and the type of payments or other value received, generally for public disclosure.  Subject to further legal review and statutory or regulatory clarification, which Pfizer intends to pursue, reimbursement of recruiting expenses for licensed physicians may constitute a reportable transfer of value under the federal transparency law commonly known as the Sunshine Act.  Therefore, if you are a licensed physician who incurs recruiting expenses as a result of interviewing with Pfizer that we pay or reimburse, your name, address and the amount of payments made currently will be reported to the government.  If you have questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to contact your Talent Acquisition representative. EEO & Employment Eligibility Pfizer is committed to equal opportunity in the terms and conditions of employment for all employees and job applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, gender identity or gender expression, national origin, disability or veteran status.  Pfizer also complies with all applicable national, state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment as well as work authorization and employment eligibility verification requirements of the Immigration and Nationality Act and IRCA.  Pfizer is an E-Verify employer. Research and Development

## 2022-02-09

*09:55:29 - @Andrea Rau has joined the channel*
**Andrea Rau** (09:57:15):
> Hi all, there is an open permanent researcher position in my team at INRAE (in Jouy en Josas, France)! If you have a PhD and are excited about data science + statistical learning + artificial intelligence + omics data + agroecology, this is a **fantastic** opportunity.
> 
> Details here:[https://t.co/eWKRoNwp20](https://t.co/eWKRoNwp20)Applications are now being accepted in the open competition until **March 8**. For more information, a webinar is being hosted on February 17 @ 14:00 Paris time:[https://t.co/tZLkuJG2AO](https://t.co/tZLkuJG2AO)Please retweet or forward to interested researchers in your networks, and don't hesitate to get in touch for more information!
  - Attachment (INRAE Jobs): [Junior Research Scientist in statistical learning and artificial intelligence](https://jobs.inrae.fr/en/open-competitions/open-competitions-research-scientists-job-profiles-crcn/cr-2022-ga-1)
    > CR-2022-GA-1 - You will join the INRAE mixed research unit Animal Genetics and Integrative Biology (GABI, 130 permanent staff members, GA Division, “Animal Genetics”), whose research aims to understand and exploit the genetic variability of domestic animals in a context of agroecological transition. Within this unit, you will join the GiBBS "Genomics, Biodiversity, Bioinformatics, Statistics" team, in which several researchers specialize in the analysis of complex and heterogeneous “omics” data. To develop your research project, you will share your time between this team and the SOLsTIS “Statistical modeling and Learning for environmenT and lIfe Sciences” team in the MIA-Paris mixed research unit (Mathematics and Applied Computer Science-Paris, 32 permanent staff members) in the MATHNUM (Mathematics and Digital Science) division, also located on the Saclay site. The research of MIA-Paris aims to develop advanced statistical and computer science methods for specific problems in the life sciences. The SOLsTIS team includes several researchers who are experts in statistical learning algorithms, including artificial intelligence. Your primary research activity will be to carry out methodological developments in data science, artificial intelligence and statistical learning for the genetic and genomic analysis of traits linked to the agroecological transition of breeding farms and their adaptation to climate change (resilience, animal welfare and adaptation, reduction of environmental impact) and the value of genetic diversity. You will benefit from the scientific environment and the support of the statisticians of the two teams GIBBS and SOLsTIS, and will thus strengthen their common research dynamic, organized in particular around the analysis of heterogeneous and large-scale data with a view to a better understanding and characterization of domestic animal phenotypes. You will design new methods or improve existing approaches for integrating heterogeneous data (multi-omics, high-throughput and continuously collected measurements, images, captor recordings, geolocation data). You will work in complementarity with GiBBS scientists, who have expertise in multivariate analysis and network inference, and SOLsTIS scientists, who will bring their skills for artificial intelligence and machine learning algorithms. You will be involved upon arrival in two projects related to animal welfare and animal adaptation to different environments. You will interact with geneticists from the GABI Unit and the GA department for statistical and data science aspects during the design and development of research projects, and for the interpretation of their results. In line with INRAE guidelines for Open Science, you will promote your research work to the scientific community through publications and create R / Python packages in order to allow a wide distribution of the developed methods. You will have access to the computing clusters of the INRAE sites in Jouy-en-Josas and Toulouse (Migale, Genotoul). You will rely on the existing network of collaborations established by the GiBBS and SOLsTIS teams and expand it at different levels: local, national, and international. You will intervene in training (masters, research schools) and supervision of interns and doctoral students.

## 2022-02-11

**Janani Ravi** (18:18:05):
> #postdoc #academia #computational_biology #bio_datascience
> 
> Hello all,**TL;DR**Our shop is now*fully *open for business, and we are looking for postdocs to join our group! We are also actively recruiting grad students and scientific programmers (descriptions in a separate post).
> Please spread the word! The link to the MSU job posting is[here](https://careers.msu.edu/en-us/job/509488/research-associatefixed-term). Please reach out to me if you have any further questions/clarifications reg. ant of these positions:[janani@msu.edu](mailto:janani@msu.edu)****+++ more about the position +++ ******Pasting the most relevant parts of the ad inline below:*****Postdoctoral Researcher | Research Associate***We are looking for a talented and motivated individual to join the JRaviLab (PI: Janani Ravi) as a postdoctoral researcher. In our group, we develop/apply computational approaches to analyze and visualize multiscale biological data to study microbial phenotypes and diseases, including genomes, protein sequence-structural features, transcriptomes, and pathway memberships. 
> Postdocs will contribute to this vision by 1) developing new computational approaches to analyze and integrate large collections of biological data, 2) applying these approaches to specific problems in microbiology and infectious diseases, and 3) communicating the methods and discoveries openly to the broader biomedical/clinical community.
> In addition to participating in ongoing projects, we strongly encourage postdocs to come up with several research ideas, jointly develop them into creative projects, and lead the way in those new directions.
>  
> We are committed to building a diverse team and sustaining an inclusive and supportive environment; we strongly encourage underrepresented minorities in STEM to apply, including but not limited to BIPOC, LGBTQIA+ folks. The position is open to international applicants, in addition to US citizens/permanent residents.***What will you get***
> 1. Freedom to work on your best ideas broadly aligned with the lab’s vision.
> 2. Several opportunities to collaborate with other smart computational and experimental researchers at MSU & elsewhere.
> 3. Resources to develop your own research program and your technical skills as you plan to move to the next stage of your career.
> 4. Opportunities to mentor students, participate in peer-review and grant-writing, and, based on your interests, participate in teaching, education, and outreach activities.
> 5. A vibrant, supportive environment: A bunch of really smart and fun colleagues; Office in a fancy central building with easy access to cafeterias; Beautiful campus; Low cost-of-living; supportive department environment and postdoctoral association (PDA) for professional development; Competitive salary commensurate with experience.
> 6. Mentoring & support: In addition to being your scientific mentor, my primary responsibility is to help your personal and professional growth by facilitating all of the above. You will always find in me an audience to discuss opportunities/challenges and a strong advocate for your interests. You will also have the option to form a formal mentoring committee.
> 7. Inclusive, friendly, and supportive space for underrepresented minorities in STEM and quantitative sciences.
> ***Qualifications***
> 1. A PhD or equivalent, with a strong background in bioinformatics, computational biology, genetics/microbiology, statistics, computer science, machine learning, or other closely related fields.
> 2. Programming experience in R or Python w/ version control (Git). Experience with high-performance cluster computing is a plus. Familiarity with bioinformatics databases and tools would also be a bonus.
> 3. Good communication skills, willingness to collaborate openly, give/take constructive feedback, and sustain a friendly and fun lab culture.
> ***More about the position******Department(s)***: Pathobiology and Diagnostic Investigation, Microbiology & Molecular Genetics***College affiliation(s)***: Veterinary Medicine, Natural Science***Location***: Michigan State University, East Lansing, MI (BPS building)***Salary***: $52K or above (commensurate with experience)***Duration***: 2–3Y with an annually renewable contract contingent upon satisfactory performance***Apply | Find out more***If you have questions or want to chat about this position, please write to me**** ****([janani@msu.edu](mailto:janani@msu.edu)). If you are interested in something like this, it’s never too early to reach out!
>  
> To apply,***apply to this posting***with 1) a detailed CV that includes a link to your Google Scholar page and a list of publications, 2) a cover letter that includes a description of your research accomplishments, how your interests connect with our group’s work, what might be some lines of future work that you are interested in, and what your future professional goals are, and 3) names & contact information for three references.
> 
> Qs? Please DM me@Janani Ravi

Together, we are leveraging a transformative shift in the understanding of cellular biology to discover and advance breakthrough therapeutics for the … Continued

It's a great opportunity for recent PhD graduates that love academia (including some teaching, presenting, lots of collaboration and literature research) but don't want to go the usual post-doc route. **Mahmoud Ahmed** (07:56:37) (in thread):
> Hi Friederike. I am curious as to how this job differs from the traditional postdoc in the day to day work. The job description in the link is very interesting, to me, but do you think it would be a step forward for someone who ultimately want to join a university faculty. Thanks for sharing the opportunity.

**Friederike Dündar** (08:01:19) (in thread):
> Good question. It certainly depends on the type of faculty position you're envisioning. Most typical tenure-tracks will require you to have a well defined research profile along which to build up your own group. For these types of positions, this would probably not be a good starting point as we're mostly involved in translating other people's visions into actionable experiments. However, there are faculty positions in the core facility environment, particularly in the US, and this would certainly be a fantastic preparation for these types of more service-oriented positions. Probably not as glamorous, but def needed:slightly_smiling_face:

**Friederike Dündar** (08:01:46) (in thread):
> Feel free to share your goals/views via PM, happy to chat more

**Mahmoud Ahmed** (17:44:46):
> Our lab is hiring (MS/PhD students, or Postdocs). We are looking for someone with a math, statistics, programming, or bioinformatics background. The lab has a mix of established wet and dry projects. We use gene expression data to explore cancer cell behavior and have a particular interest in autophagy. We develop tools for working with gene expression and regulation. We use high-throughput data (RNA/ChIP-seq) to develop hypotheses and test them experimentally. The student/postdoc will work on existing projects and will also have a chance to initiate their own.[https://www.researchgate.net/lab/Deok-Ryong-Kim-Lab](https://www.researchgate.net/lab/Deok-Ryong-Kim-Lab)[https://www.gnu.ac.kr/eng/main.do](https://www.gnu.ac.kr/eng/main.do)

> Our team at Seattle Children's is looking to hire a data scientist to join our core team.  Please reach out to us if you have interest![https://careers-seattlechildrens.icims.com/jobs/43681/data-scientist-sr./job?mode=view&mobile=\[…\]needsRedirect=false&jan1offset=-480&jun1offset=-420](https://careers-seattlechildrens.icims.com/jobs/43681/data-scientist-sr./job?mode=view&mobile=false&width=743&height=500&bga=true&needsRedirect=false&jan1offset=-480&jun1offset=-420)
  - Attachment (careers): [Data Scientist Sr. in Seattle, Washington | Careers at 818 Stewart](https://careers-seattlechildrens.icims.com/jobs/43681/data-scientist-sr./job?mode=view&amp;mobile=false&amp;width=743&amp;height=500&amp;bga=true&amp;needsRedirect=false&amp;jan1offset=-480&amp;jun1offset=-420)
    > We are actively seeking a Computational Biologist/Data Scientist with flexibility for our top candidate to reside in Washington, Alaska, Montana, Florida, Georgia, or Texas.   As part of the Research Scientific Computing Team, you'll work with scientists, clinicians, developers, analysts, and business owners in a team-driven, agile environment to develop and scale bioinformatics and data science results and products. Using traditional bioinformatics applications combined with modern big data technologies, our team enables the Institution to better leverage high throughput multi-omics analyses to accelerate translational research and improve patient care. Collaborate with researchers across multiple disciplines to develop solutions and tools to advance research with advanced analytics. Provide support and training to researcher partners to improve technical literacy and programming skills across the organization. Dive deep into vast repositories of structured, unstructured, and streaming data to find the input for models. Create tools and libraries that can leverage products for efficient use across the enterprise and beyond.

**Kellie Kravarik** (10:56:53):
> Come work with biologists who think about cutting edge bench work and computational analysis at the same time! Systems Immunology at Pfizer is looking for a biologist/immunologist to focus on genome editing of immune cells in order to generate high-throughput single-cell multiomic data to support our remit of identifying new drug targets and supporting our pipeline. We are a group who think ‘damp’ (wet & dry lab work) all the time, and we’re looking for new folks who can bridge the worlds of the cell editing in the lab and scMultiomics data in the laptop![https://pfizer.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/PfizerCareers/job/United-States---Massachusetts---Cambridge/Senior-Scientist---Systems-Immunology---Cellular-Genomics_4845993-1](https://pfizer.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/PfizerCareers/job/United-States---Massachusetts---Cambridge/Senior-Scientist---Systems-Immunology---Cellular-Genomics_4845993-1)
  - Attachment (pfizer.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com): [Senior Scientist,  Systems Immunology - Cellular Genomics](https://pfizer.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/PfizerCareers/job/United-States---Massachusetts---Cambridge/Senior-Scientist---Systems-Immunology---Cellular-Genomics_4845993-1)
    > What You Will Achieve The Inflammation and Immunology Research Unit is seeking a dynamic individual to join the Systems Immunology group and support next-generation cellular and functional genomics using single-cell multiomics. This person will be responsible for the genome editing and cellular engineering of immune and stromal cells, to support arrayed, pooled and in vivo cellular phenotyping. They will execute technical and laboratory aspects of in-house functional genomics projects, including the design and execution of laboratory experiments. The candidate will contribute to analysis and interpretation of high dimensional cellular profiling data. Finally, the candidate will be expected to provide training and technical guidance to colleagues in a matrix environment as a systems immunology expert. How You Will Achieve It Lead and execute Systems Immunology’s lab-based experimental efforts in functional genomics enabled immunophenotyping Design, execute, and interpret pooled and arrayed CRISPR experiments in collaboration with the Systems Immunology and Target Sciences group to phenotype the functional contribution of disease-associated genes using in vitro human cellular systems and in vivo animal studies Generate and analyze pooled single-cell functional genomic studies from experimental conception to cellular editing and single-cell multiomic profiling, across a diverse array of immune cell types Develop new functional genomic methods to enable high dimensional and high throughput immune phenotyping in challenging cell types, organs, and tissues Effectively utilize available resources as needed to achieve timely execution of projects Work with cross-functional teams to execute plans and deliverables Qualifications Must-Have PhD in Immunology, Biology, or other related discipline (or equivalent years of experience) with 0-3 years post-PhD experience in an academic or biopharma/biotech setting Expertise in cell culture of immune cells, and cellular models of immunity Demonstrated expertise in CRISPR or other genome editing technologies Demonstrated expertise in gene and vector delivery using viral and non-viral methods Demonstrated expertise in pooled or arrayed functional genomic screens 3+ years of hands-on molecular biology and genome editing experience 1+ year of experience in CRISPR technologies and/or pooled/arrayed screens Working understanding of analysis methods for high/medium throughput screens Nice-to-Have 1+ year of hands-on knowledge and experience with PerturbSeq/pooled CRISPR screens with single cell phenotyping Hands-on knowledge and experience with in vivo pooled screening 1+ year of experience with computational and statistical approaches for high-dimensional data analysis, especially single-cell RNAseq #LI-PFE Pfizer requires all U.S. new hires to be fully vaccinated for COVID-19 prior to the first date of employment. As required by applicable law, Pfizer will consider requests for Reasonable Accommodations. Sunshine Act Pfizer reports payments and other transfers of value to health care providers as required by federal and state transparency laws and implementing regulations. These laws and regulations require Pfizer to provide government agencies with information such as a health care provider’s name, address and the type of payments or other value received, generally for public disclosure. Subject to further legal review and statutory or regulatory clarification, which Pfizer intends to pursue, reimbursement of recruiting expenses for licensed physicians may constitute a reportable transfer of value under the federal transparency law commonly known as the Sunshine Act. Therefore, if you are a licensed physician who incurs recruiting expenses as a result of interviewing with Pfizer that we pay or reimburse, your name, address and the amount of payments made currently will be reported to the government. If you have questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to contact your Talent Acquisition representative. EEO & Employment Eligibility Pfizer is committed to equal opportunity in the terms and conditions of employment for all employees and job applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, gender identity or gender expression, national origin, disability or veteran status.
